data.output:
1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: FEAT-005
4	  depends_on:
5	    - helix.prd
6	    - FEAT-007
7	---
8	# Feature: Artifact Convention
9	
10	**ID:** FEAT-005
11	**Status:** In Progress
12	**Priority:** P1
13	**Owner:** DDx Team
14	
15	## Overview
16	
17	An **artifact** is any file carrying DDx **identity** — a structured `id` plus
18	optional graph and provenance metadata. Identity may be embedded (in a
19	markdown file's YAML frontmatter under a `ddx:` block) or supplied via a
20	sidecar `<filename>.ddx.yaml` for non-markdown media (diagrams, wireframes,
21	images, prompts, audio, PDFs, binaries, etc.).
22	
23	The `id` field identifies the artifact; the ID prefix conventionally
24	indicates the type (ADR-001, SD-003, MET-007, FEAT-012, etc.). DDx does
25	not hardcode artifact types — it manages the document graph generically.
26	Types are conventions that workflows define.
27	
28	This replaces the previous approach of dedicated `ddx adr` and `ddx sd` commands. Those are removed in favor of the generic `ddx doc` commands (FEAT-007).
29	
30	## Definition
31	
32	### Authority rule
33	
34	**Identity present → artifact.** A file is an artifact if and only if DDx can
35	resolve identity for it, by either of two mechanisms:
36	
37	1. **Embedded identity** — markdown files with a `ddx:` block in YAML
38	   frontmatter:
39	
40	   ```yaml
41	   ---
42	   ddx:
43	     id: <PREFIX>-<NNN>
44	     depends_on: [<other-ids>]
45	   ---
46	   # Content
47	   ```
48	
49	2. **Sidecar identity** — any file `<path>/<name>.<ext>` accompanied by
50	   `<path>/<name>.<ext>.ddx.yaml` containing the identity block:
51	
52	   ```yaml
53	   # diagram.excalidraw.ddx.yaml
54	   id: DIAG-007
55	   media_type: application/vnd.excalidraw+json
56	   depends_on: [SD-003]
57	   generated_by:
58	     run: <run-id>
59	     prompt: <input-selector>
60	     source_hash: <sha256>
61	   ```
62	
63	There is no second-class "managed asset" concept. Either a file has identity
64	(and is an artifact), or it does not (and DDx ignores it for graph purposes).
65	
66	DDx discovers artifacts by scanning for embedded frontmatter and sidecar
67	`*.ddx.yaml` files, not by looking in specific directories.
68	
69	### Common Artifact Types (by convention)
70	
71	| Prefix | Type | Typical Location |
72	|--------|------|-----------------|
73	| `FEAT` | Feature specification | `docs/helix/01-frame/features/` |
74	| `ADR` | Architecture Decision Record | `docs/adr/` or `docs/helix/02-design/adr/` |
75	| `SD` | Solution Design | `docs/designs/` or `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/` |
76	| `TD` | Technical Design | `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/` |
77	| `TP` | Test Plan | `docs/helix/03-test/` |
78	| `MET` | Metric definition | `docs/metrics/` |
79	| `US` | User Story | `docs/helix/01-frame/user-stories/` |
80	| `DIAG` | Diagram (mermaid, excalidraw, svg) | `docs/diagrams/` |
81	| `WIRE` | Wireframe / mockup | `docs/wireframes/` |
82	| `IMG` | Image asset (hero, illustration) | `docs/images/` |
83	| `PRMT` | Prompt artifact | `docs/prompts/` |
84	
85	These prefixes are conventions — DDx treats them all the same. Workflows
86	(HELIX) may enforce that certain types exist or follow specific templates.
87	
88	Projects may introduce additional conventions such as `AC-*` for acceptance
89	criteria or `TC-*` for test cases. DDx does not privilege those types; it only
90	tracks their IDs and relationships.
91	
92	### Identity Schema
93	
94	Identity carries the same field set whether embedded under a markdown
95	`ddx:` block or stored in a sidecar `.ddx.yaml`.
96	
97	| Field | Type | Required | Description |
98	|-------|------|----------|-------------|
99	| `id` | string | yes | Unique identifier with type prefix (e.g., `ADR-001`) |
100	| `media_type` | string | conditional | IANA-style media type. Required for sidecar identity; inferred from extension for embedded markdown (`text/markdown`). |
101	| `depends_on` | []string | no | IDs of artifacts this one depends on |
102	| `inputs` | []string | no | Input selectors for prompt context resolution |
103	| `generated_by` | object | no | Provenance for generated artifacts: `{ run, prompt, source_hash }`. Records the layer-1/layer-2 run that produced this artifact and the hash of its source inputs. |
104	| `review` | object | no | Staleness tracking (managed by `ddx doc stamp`) |
105	| `prompt` | string | no | Custom prompt for updating this document |
106	| `parking_lot` | bool | no | Skip in staleness checks |
107	
108	Unknown fields are preserved on round-trip.
109	
110	#### `media_type`
111	
112	Identifies how an artifact's bytes should be interpreted. Examples:
113	
114	- `text/markdown` (default for embedded identity)
115	- `image/png`, `image/svg+xml`
116	- `application/vnd.excalidraw+json`
117	- `application/pdf`
118	- `text/mermaid`
119	- `application/vnd.ddx.prompt+yaml`
120	
121	Web UI rendering, regenerate flows, and tooling key off `media_type` rather
122	than file extension.
123	
124	#### `generated_by`
125	
126	Marks an artifact as the output of a DDx run. Carries the run id, the prompt
127	selector, and the `source_hash` used at generation time. Regeneration is
128	source-hash-driven: when inputs change, the source hash diverges and tooling
129	can offer to regenerate. `generated_by` creates a separate provenance edge in
130	the graph (FEAT-007) — it tracks origin, not authority, and does **not**
131	cascade staleness like `depends_on`.
132	
133	## The Artifact Graph
134	
135	All artifacts in a project — markdown and non-markdown alike — form a
136	**single directed acyclic graph** via `depends_on` edges. This graph is the
137	project's authority structure: it encodes which artifacts govern which.
138	
139	```
140	Product Vision
141	  └─ PRD
142	       ├─ FEAT-001
143	       │    ├─ SD-001
144	       │    │    ├─ TD-001
145	       │    │    │    └─ TP-001
146	       │    │    └─ DIAG-001  (excalidraw, sidecar identity)
147	       │    └─ US-001
148	       ├─ FEAT-002
149	       │    └─ ADR-001
150	       └─ MET-001
151	```
152	
153	Every artifact should be reachable from a root (typically the vision or PRD). Orphaned artifacts — those with no incoming or outgoing edges — are a smell that `ddx doc validate` flags.
154	
155	### Graph Properties
156	
157	- **Single graph per project.** All artifacts with identity participate in one graph regardless of media type. There are no separate "ADR graph," "diagram graph," or "feature graph" partitions.
158	- **Edges are explicit.** Only `depends_on` creates authority edges. `generated_by` creates a parallel provenance edge that is not authority. No implicit edges from directory structure or naming.
159	- **Direction is authority.** A `depends_on` edge from B to A means "A governs B" — if A changes, B may be stale.
160	- **Authority staleness cascades.** If a node is stale, all its `depends_on` descendants are transitively stale.
161	- **Provenance staleness does not cascade.** A `generated_by` source-hash mismatch flags only the generated artifact itself, offering regeneration; it does not propagate.
162	- **Types and media are orthogonal.** The graph doesn't care about artifact types or media. An ADR can depend on a feature spec; a metric can depend on a PRD; a wireframe can depend on a solution design. The graph is type- and media-agnostic.
163	
164	## What DDx Does With Artifacts
165	
166	All via `ddx doc` commands (FEAT-007):
167	
168	1. **Discover** — scan for embedded `ddx:` frontmatter in markdown and for sidecar `*.ddx.yaml` files
169	2. **Graph** — build the single project-wide dependency DAG plus provenance edges
170	3. **Stale** — detect when upstream `depends_on` artifacts changed since last stamp; detect generated artifacts whose `source_hash` no longer matches
171	4. **Stamp** — record review hashes after updating a document
172	5. **List** — list artifacts with optional type or media filtering (`ddx doc list --type ADR`, `ddx artifact list --media-type image/png`)
173	6. **Show** — display artifact content/metadata; for non-markdown media, show the sidecar plus a media-typed pointer
174	7. **Validate** — check identity structure, dependency references exist, no circular `depends_on`, no orphans, sidecars match an existing target file
175	
176	## What DDx Does NOT Do
177	
178	- **Scaffold from templates** — agents and workflows create artifacts. DDx doesn't need `create` commands for each type.
179	- **Enforce type-specific structure** — "ADRs must have a Decision section" is a workflow concern. DDx validates the graph; dun validates content structure.
180	- **Hardcode artifact types or media** — no switch statements on type prefixes or media types. The graph treats all artifacts equally.
181	- **Partition the graph** — all artifacts are in one graph regardless of type, media, or directory.
182	- **Execute artifact files directly** — runtime execution belongs to DDx runs (FEAT-010), not the artifact graph itself.
183	- **Recognize a "managed asset" tier** — files without identity are not artifacts, period. There is no halfway state.
184	
185	## Templates and Prompts
186	
187	Each artifact type has associated resources in the document library:
188	
189	```
190	.ddx/library/artifacts/<type>/
191	├── template.md       # Structural template (frontmatter + sections), or sidecar template for binary types
192	├── create.md         # Prompt for creating a new instance
193	├── evolve.md         # Prompt for updating when dependencies change
194	└── check.md          # Prompt for reviewing/validating the artifact
195	```
196	
197	For example, `.ddx/library/artifacts/adr/`:
198	- `template.md` — ADR skeleton with Context/Decision/Alternatives sections
199	- `create.md` — prompt instructing an agent how to write a good ADR
200	- `evolve.md` — prompt for updating an ADR when upstream requirements change
201	- `check.md` — prompt for reviewing an ADR against its governing artifacts
202	
203	These are **library resources**, not CLI features. Agents and workflows read them from the library when operating on artifacts. DDx ships defaults; projects can override with project-specific versions.
204	
205	The doc graph (FEAT-007) can resolve these prompts via input selectors:
206	- `artifact-prompt:ADR:create` → the create prompt for ADR artifacts
207	- `artifact-template:ADR` → the template for ADR artifacts
208	
209	## Artifacts and Runs
210	
211	Artifacts stay declarative. They define meaning, authority, provenance, and
212	relationships in the graph. DDx does **not** execute artifact files directly.
213	
214	Runtime invocation belongs to the three-layer run architecture (FEAT-010):
215	`ddx run` (layer-1 single agent invocation), `ddx try <bead>` (layer-2 bead
216	attempt), `ddx work` (layer-3 queue drain). Run records are file-backed and
217	linked to artifacts by ID via `generated_by`.
218	
219	### Boundary
220	
221	- Artifacts declare what matters, who governs it, where it came from, and how
222	  it relates to other artifacts in the graph.
223	- Runs describe one invocation that may produce or consume artifacts.
224	- Run records capture one immutable invocation: raw logs, structured result
225	  data, status, tokens/duration/exit, and provenance.
226	- Runs and run records are not artifacts and do not participate in document
227	  staleness or graph validation.
228	- DDx does not infer run semantics from an artifact type prefix.
229	
230	### Examples
231	
232	- A `MET-*` artifact may link to a layer-1 run definition that emits numeric
233	  observations over time.
234	- A `DIAG-*` excalidraw artifact may carry `generated_by` pointing at the
235	  layer-1 run that produced it; regenerating updates the file and refreshes
236	  the source hash.
237	- Metric comparison and trend views are projections over runs, not evidence
238	  stored in a separate metric-owned runtime backend.
239	- A project may model acceptance criteria or test cases as standalone
240	  artifacts and link them to runs that emit pass/fail or richer structured
241	  results.
242	- A run may evaluate multiple governing artifacts together as long as those
243	  links are explicit in the run record.
244	
245	The shared rule is simple: artifact identity establishes meaning, authority,
246	and provenance; DDx runs establish repeatable runtime behavior and evidence.
247	
248	## Migration
249	
250	The dedicated `ddx adr` and `ddx sd` commands are removed. Their functionality is subsumed by:
251	
252	| Old Command | Replacement |
253	|-------------|-------------|
254	| `ddx adr create` | Agent creates file from template |
255	| `ddx adr list` | `ddx doc list --type ADR` |
256	| `ddx adr show ADR-001` | `ddx doc show ADR-001` |
257	| `ddx adr validate` | `ddx doc validate` |
258	| `ddx sd create` | Agent creates file from template |
259	| `ddx sd list` | `ddx doc list --type SD` |
260	| `ddx sd show SD-001` | `ddx doc show SD-001` |
261	| `ddx sd validate` | `ddx doc validate` |
262	
263	Existing markdown artifacts are unaffected by the multi-media broadening:
264	their embedded `ddx:` identity continues to work and `media_type` defaults
265	to `text/markdown` when absent. Adoption of sidecar identity is opt-in,
266	file by file.
267	
268	## Dependencies
269	
270	- FEAT-007 (Doc Graph) — provides the `ddx doc` commands, sidecar scanner,
271	  `media_type` handling, and `generated_by` provenance edges
272	- FEAT-010 (Runs) — provides the run records that `generated_by` references
273	- Document library with templates
274	
275	## Out of Scope
276	
277	- Type-specific CLI commands (use `ddx doc` generically)
278	- Content structure validation (that's dun/workflow-level)
279	- Template application commands (agents do this)
280	- A "managed asset" or any other second-class artifact tier
281

| `PRMT` | Prompt artifact | `docs/prompts/` |
| `RSCH` | Research note | `docs/helix/00-discover/research/` |
| `REF` | Reference (external source capture) | `docs/helix/00-discover/references/` |

data.text: Now add a frontmatter schema example showing REF metadata fields outside the `ddx:` block. I'll add it after the Identity Schema section.

input.old_string: Unknown fields are preserved on round-trip.
input.new_string:
Unknown fields are preserved on round-trip.

#### Type-specific metadata (outside `ddx:`)

Type conventions may carry additional metadata fields at the top level of
frontmatter, alongside (not inside) the `ddx:` identity block. The parser
treats these as inert pass-through fields. For example, a `REF-NNN`
reference artifact captures provenance about an external source:

```yaml
---
ddx:
  id: REF-007
  depends_on: [RSCH-003]
source_url: https://example.com/article
source_author: Jane Doe
accessed: 2026-05-01
---
# Reference: ...
```

Identity (`id`, `depends_on`, etc.) stays under `ddx:`; type-specific fields
like `source_url`, `source_author`, and `accessed` live at the top level.

| `REF` | Reference, external source capture (IDs `REF-NNN`) | `docs/helix/00-discover/references/` |
